title: Zygia apoensis
intro: Zygia apoensis is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Leguminosae, commonly known as a legume tree. Traditional medicine has utilized Zygia apoensis to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Zygia apoensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Zygia apoensis is often prepared as a decoction or tincture to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Zygia apoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the Philippines and is a type of legume. The classification of Zygia apoensis includes its kingdom as Plantae and its phylum as Magnoliophyta. Within the kingdom, it is classified as a member of the clade Angiosperms. Further classification places it in the order Fabales.
